calendar.matters:
Petition For Modification Of Trust; To Delcare The Last Will And Testament Of John M Grollmus Valid; To Authorize Trustee To Use Trust Property To Fun
Off Calendar Mar-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christina Gallagher Nelson for (petitioner) Laura Grollmus.

The Court stated that it is struggling to figure out what is being asked for and what the plan is, that it is not sure what would be accomplished if the court amends the trust if assets will not be in the trust. Ms. Nelson stated that previous counsel had filed a petition to have the will admitted and bequests distributed and was told that it could not be done by the probate examiner. The Court stated that all of the significant assets are in joint accounts. Ms. Nelson stated that $14,000.00 is not properly titled. The Court stated that it is not properly titled in the estate, that you do not need a probate for $14,000.00, that the will says if there is $600,000.00 in the estate then the gifts kick in but they are not in the trust. Ms. Nelson stated that the beneficiaries were going to challenge, that there is potential for conflict, that counsel changed, that when talking to opposing counsel it was clear they were on the fence and that if they were not going to probate the only other theory was to get confirmation of the trust. The Court stated that there is a petition to amend the trust but that there are no assets in the trust, that it seems pointless and inappropriate, that the terms are already in the will and that it is not clear why the trust would be amended. Ms. Nelson stated that it is the only legal theory for the trustee to use the assets. The Court stated that to give advisory on a fight that is not before the court is not appropriate, that there is nothing in the trust to distribute, that there is nothing indicating how anything would get into the trust, that the trust was create but not funded, that the will was created but everything is in joint accounts or pay on death accounts and that the trustee can just give the gifts as she already has the assets. Ms. Nelson stated that if they collect under a Small Estate it would create a gift tax issue. The Court stated that it will deny the petition, that it does not think it can do what is requested and that it does not make sense. Ms. Nelson stated that she assumes everyone would be agreeable to a dismissal. The Court dismissed the petition for modification of trust; to declare the last will and testament of John J. Grollmus valid; to authorize trustee to use trust property to fund trust with prejudice.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
